grounding_source_rows: gold FINDINGS mechanism 3 (missing party/procedural facts); taxonomy Informal Living Arrangements description
grounding_note: 'My partner and I are splitting up' routes completely differently depending on whether the couple is legally married: divorce law divides property and awards support; unmarried cohabitants get Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements / property law. Marital status is the single most basic fact intake can ask, and openings genuinely omit it ('partner', 'ex', 'we').

scenarios.opening_query: My partner and I are splitting up after twelve years and I have no idea how we untangle everything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements
scenarios.hidden_fact: We never married — no ceremony, no registration — just twelve years, a house in his name, and joint accounts.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We never actually married — twelve years together, house in his name, joint accounts, but no marriage.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the couple is legally married
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We married at the courthouse in 2014 — it's a real divorce with everything that entails.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We married at the courthouse in 2014 — it's a real divorce.
scenarios.vetting_note: The purest version of FINDINGS mechanism 3; 'partner' is genuinely status-ambiguous in modern usage.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex is keeping the house we bought together and says I have no rights to it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Real Property > General (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: We're actually still legally married — separated for three years but nobody ever filed, so this belongs in a divorce case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We're still legally married — separated three years but no one ever filed. So it's a divorce matter.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a legal marriage still exists
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We never married — we bought it as boyfriend and girlfriend, both names on the loan but only hers on the deed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Never married — bought it as a couple, both on the loan, only her on the deed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Long-separated-but-married is a real trap; filing posture flips the entire framework.

scenarios.opening_query: I supported my partner through medical school and now that we're done I'm left with nothing.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We never married — eight years of me paying our bills on a promise, but no legal marriage ever happened.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We never married — eight years of me carrying the bills on a promise, but no marriage.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: marital status and available property/support theories
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We married before she started school — my lawyer friend says that supporting her career is exactly what compensatory spousal support covers.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We married before she started school — I'm told that's exactly what compensatory support is for.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same sacrifice story lands in contract/equity law or compensatory support depending on the status fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My girlfriend moved out and took things that we bought together, including the dog.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || General Litigation > Animal Law || Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers
scenarios.hidden_fact: Honestly the property doesn't matter — it's the dog. I bought him, chipped him in my name, and want custody of him resolved.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The stuff doesn't matter — it's the dog. He's chipped in my name and I bought him. I want that resolved.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the pet dispute is the actual claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the whole household — furniture, the car we split payments on, and $6,000 from our shared account.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's everything — furniture, the car we both paid on, and $6,000 from our joint account.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pet-custody is the taxonomy's own Animal Law example; scope disclosure routes it.

scenarios.opening_query: My fiancé wants me to sign papers about money before the wedding and I'm uneasy about it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Prenuptial Agreements || Family Law > Document Review
scenarios.hidden_fact: The papers are already drafted by his attorney and the wedding is in five weeks — I need my own independent review before signing, which his lawyer can't provide.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His attorney already drafted it and the wedding's in five weeks — I need my own independent review before signing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the need is independent review of a drafted agreement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's drafted — we both want a fair prenup written from scratch together.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing exists yet — we want a fair prenup drafted from scratch.
scenarios.vetting_note: Draft-vs-review posture; independent-counsel review has its own service label.

scenarios.opening_query: My boyfriend's name is on our lease and he's telling me I have to be out by Sunday.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Informal Living Arrangements || Real Property > Tenant (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I've paid half the rent by bank transfer for three years and get mail there — a tenant hotline told me I have tenancy rights and he can't just throw me out.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I've paid half the rent by transfer for three years and get mail there — the hotline says I have tenant rights he can't ignore.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the partner has established tenancy rights in the home
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I only moved in two months ago and haven't paid toward rent — the fight is really about our shared savings for the trip and the furniture I bought.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I moved in two months ago and haven't paid rent — the real fight is our shared savings and my furniture.
scenarios.vetting_note: Cohabitant-as-tenant is a genuine dual frame; the payment/duration facts pick the housing route.
